step_by_step_thinking:
I agree that FNAC may fall short in diagnosing some forms of breast cancer due to its limitations with larger, more variable tissues. However, another important consideration is the presence of calcifications within breast cancers, where FNAC might miss subtle details crucial for diagnosis. This further supports the idea that FNAC alone isn't sufficient for comprehensive evaluation of all breast carcinomas.
